root_text.an5.84:1.1: “Pañcahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato thero bhikkhu sabrahmacārīnaṁ appiyo ca hoti, amanāpo ca agaru ca abhāvanīyo ca.
root_text.an5.84:1.3: Assaddho hoti, ahiriko hoti, anottappī hoti, kusīto hoti, duppañño hoti—
root_text.an5.84:1.4: imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato thero bhikkhu sabrahmacārīnaṁ appiyo ca hoti amanāpo ca agaru ca abhāvanīyo ca.
root_text.an5.84:2.1: Pañcahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato thero bhikkhu sabrahmacārīnaṁ piyo ca hoti manāpo ca garu ca bhāvanīyo ca.
root_text.an5.84:2.3: Saddho hoti, hirīmā hoti, ottappī hoti, āraddhavīriyo hoti, paññavā hoti—
root_text.an5.84:2.4: imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato thero bhikkhu sabrahmacārīnaṁ piyo ca hoti manāpo ca garu ca bhāvanīyo cā”ti.

translation_text.an5.84:1.1: “Mendicants, a senior mendicant with five qualities is unlikable and disagreeable to their spiritual companions, not respected or admired.
translation_text.an5.84:1.3: They’re faithless, shameless, imprudent, lazy, and witless.
translation_text.an5.84:1.4: A senior mendicant with these five qualities is unlikable and disagreeable to their spiritual companions, not respected or admired.
translation_text.an5.84:2.1: A senior mendicant with five qualities is dear and beloved to their spiritual companions, respected and admired.
translation_text.an5.84:2.3: They’re faithful, conscientious, prudent, energetic, and wise.
translation_text.an5.84:2.4: A senior mendicant with these five qualities is dear and beloved to their spiritual companions, respected and admired.”
